steps.title: In the meantime, prepare 2 mL...
steps.instruction: In the meantime, prepare 2 mL Eppendorf tubes with 2-3 glass beads (4 mm) and 100 mg of tissue and transfer tubes into liquid nitrogen. This is the easiest with fresh tissue because freeze thawing is avoided during cutting. However, if there is no other way, tissue frozen in liquid nitrogen (ensuring it stays frozen) also works. 100 mg, Tissue (COOL DOWN CENTRIFUGE TO 4ºC AND HEAT UP THE THERMOBLOCK TO 37ºC FOR NEXT STEPS)

steps.title: Before grinding make sure tissue is...
steps.instruction: Before grinding make sure tissue is completely frozen in liquid nitrogen and perform the grinding steps as quickly as possible to avoid freeze thawing. The grinding rack can also be frozen to ensure that (it is highly recommended to store it at -80°C for at least 3 hours, overnight if possible). Grind the tissue using an automated grinder (Qiagen TissueLyzer II) for 30 seconds (actual grinding time may differ from tissue to tissue). Immediately put the tube into liquid nitrogen. If the tissue is not well ground, repeat this step until it is (grinding is crucial for the extraction). 00:00:30, Grinding Alternatively, grind the frozen material manually with a mortar.

steps.title: Add 700 µL of preheated buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL of preheated buffer and mix by inverting tube until no frozen clumps of tissue are left (This can take up to a few minutes, but it's worth it). 700 µL, Preheated buffer

steps.title: Measure DNA concentration with Qubit and absorbance with NanoDrop
steps.instruction: Measure DNA concentration with Qubit and absorbance with NanoDrop. A260/280 values should be between 1.8 and 2.0. A260/230 values should be between 2.0 and 2.0. If the clean up is going to be performed, Qubit measurement is not really necessary at this point.

steps.title: Transfer the DNA solutions into one...
steps.instruction: Transfer the DNA solutions into one tube and add TE buffer to make up a total volume of 500 µL. If the extraction is done with multiple tubes at a time (which is usually the case), then after the elution step all the eluted DNA can just be pipetted into one tube.

steps.title: Measure Qubit and Nanodrop values again (as in step 25)
steps.instruction: Measure Qubit and Nanodrop values again (as in step 25). An agarose gel is also highly recommended to ensure the samples are clean and that there is not DNA fragmentation.

steps.title: Always prepare fresh lysis buffer before...
steps.instruction: Always prepare fresh lysis buffer before extraction for optimal results (especially DTT should be added fresh). For 10 mL Lysis Buffer: Final Stock Input (10mL) Input (5mL) 1 1% PVP 40 (0.1g in 1ml for stock) 10% 1mL 500µL 2 1% PVP 10 (0.1g in 1ml) 10% 1mL 500µL 3 500mM NaCl (0.2922g in 1mL) 5M 1mL 500µL 4 100mM Tris pH 8 1M 1mL 500µL 5 50mM EDTA 0.5M 1mL 500µL 6 1.25% SDS (2g SDS in 100mL) 20% 625µL 312.5µL 7 1% (w/v)! Sodium metabisulfite 190.1 g/mol 0.1g 0.05g 8 5mM Dithiothreitol (DTT) (0.077g in 500µl) 1M 50 µL 12.5µL 9 Miliq Water 4.3mL 2.16mL Heat lysis buffer to 64 °C for 30 minutes. (This is important for the DNase heat inactivation). After cooling down to room temperature, add per 1 mL lysis buffer 1 μL RNase A (in this case 10μL or 5μL). 00:30:00, Heating to 64ºC 10 µL, RNAse A

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C in a thermomixer...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C in a thermomixer shaking at 400 rpm (slowly) for 20 minutes. 00:20:00, Incubation at 37ºC

steps.title: Take the tubes out of the...
steps.instruction: Take the tubes out of the thermomixer and cool down on ice for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, Cool down on ice

steps.title: Add 10 µL proteinase K per...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL proteinase K per tube and incubate for another 30 min at 37°C in the thermomixer. 10 µL, Proteinase K 00:30:00, Incubation at 37ºC

steps.title: Add 210 µL (~0
steps.instruction: Add 210 µL (~0.3 volumes) of 5M potassium acetate and mix by inverting the tube 20 times. Then immediately keep on ice at ~4°C for 10 minutes. 210 µL, 5M Potassium Acetate 00:10:00, on ice (4ºC)

steps.title: Centrifuge at 6000g for 20 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 6000g for 20 minutes at 4°C. 6000 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant (~600 µL) to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant (~600 µL) to a new 1.5 mL tube without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 1 volume (~600 µL) of...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume (~600 µL) of the bead solution (make sure beads are at room temperature and well homogenized by vortexing for approximately 30 seconds). 600 µL, Beads solution 00:00:30, Vortex (VORTEX THE BEADS ALONE, DO NOT VORTEX THE SAMPLES CONTAINING THE DNA)

steps.title: Mix by inversion and then incubate...
steps.instruction: Mix by inversion and then incubate on a rotor for 10 minutes at RT. 00:10:00, Incubation on rotor (In the meantime PREHEAT TE-BUFFER AT 50°C )

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 1 second.

steps.title: Place the tube in a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube in a magnetic rack for 5 minutes (until beads are stuck to the wall of the tube and solution becomes clear). 00:05:00, Magnetic rack

steps.title: Remove the supernatant without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of fresh 70...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of fresh 70 % ethanol and wait for 30 seconds. 1 mL, 70% Ethanol 00:00:30, Wait

steps.title: Remove supernatant without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Repeat the washing steps 15 - 16 once again
steps.instruction: Repeat the washing steps 15 - 16 once again.

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 1...
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 1 second and place the tube on the magnetic rack to remove the remaining ethanol.

steps.title: Let the beads air-dry for 30...
steps.instruction: Let the beads air-dry for 30 seconds, but not longer because this would decrease elution efficiency. 00:00:30, Air drying

steps.title: Add 50 µL (can be changed...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL (can be changed to the amount needed, between 20 and 100 µL is recommended) of TE buffer preheated to 50°C and resuspend the beads by flicking the tube (ensure they are no longer aggregated). 50 µL, TE buffer

steps.title: Incubate the resuspended beads for 10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate the resuspended beads for 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00, Incubation at RT

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 1...
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 1 second and place the tube in the magnetic rack and incubate for 5 -10 minutes (until solution becomes clear). 00:10:00, Magnetic rack

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant (eluted DNA) into new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant (eluted DNA) into new tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL (1 V) chloroform:isoamylalcohol...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL (1 V) chloroform:isoamylalcohol (24:1) and invert tube about 100 times (2 minutes at least). 500 µL, Chloroform:Isoamylalcohol (24:1) 00:02:00, Invertion mix

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes to separate the...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes to separate the phases at 8000g at 4°C for 10 minutes. If the supernatant is still cloudy centrifuge it again at 6000g at 4°C for 10 minutes. 8000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes 6000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes, if still cloudy

steps.title: Transfer the upper phase (DNA) into...
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper phase (DNA) into a new tube and discard the lower chloroform phase. (Depending on how well the transfer worked out, one round is usually enough but if some of the intermediate phase has been transferred steps 28 -29 can be repeated. In this case centrifuge only once in step 28).

steps.title: Add 50 µL (0
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL (0.1 V) 3 M sodium acetate (NaAc) and mix by inverting tube 10-20 times. 50 µL, 3 M Sodium Acetate

steps.title: Add 1 mL (2 V) of...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL (2 V) of ice cold (-20°C) 100% ethanol and mix by inverting tube carefully a few times. Then, incubate at -20°C overnight. (Alternatively, samples can be incubated for 1 or 2 hours and perform the DNA extraction in just one day. Longer precipitation gets more DNA but worse purity). 1 mL, 100% Ethanol Overnight, Incubatuin at -20ºC

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5000g and 4°C for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5000g and 4°C for 5 minutes. 5000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes (In the meantime PREHEAT UP TE BUFFER UP TO 50°C )

steps.title: Pipette supernatant off and make sure...
steps.instruction: Pipette supernatant off and make sure to do it on the opposite side from the pellet (usually transparent) is supposed to be. Save the supernatant and centrifuge it at 5000g and 4°C for 10 minutes. Then continue the following steps of the extraction using them as extra samples (supernatants can be stored at 4°C or -20°C and be used after extraction just in case no pellet was obtained from step 32). 5000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Add 1 mL ice cold 70...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL ice cold 70 % ethanol to pellet to wash off salt. 1 µL, 70% Ethanol at 4ºC

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4500g and 4°C for 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4500g and 4°C for 15 minutes. 4500 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat steps 35 - 37 once...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 35 - 37 once again and make sure all ethanol is pipetted off.

steps.title: Let air dry (for the last...
steps.instruction: Let air dry (for the last removal of ethanol) for 2 - 5 minutes, or until no residue of ethanol remains in the tubes. 00:05:00, Air drying (Here, drying time is not as critical as in the beads drying step as the DNA pellet should easier dissolve than the beads)

steps.title: Add 50 µL of preheated (to...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of preheated (to 50°C) TE buffer and elute DNA as long as necessary for the whole pellet to dissolve. If it's a large pellet, let it dissolve overnight at room temperature. 50 µL, TE buffer
